Annual ESOP Benefit Statement
Cover Letter — {{planYear}} Plan Year
Company
{{companyName}}
Plan Year
{{planYear}}
Recipient
{{participantName}}
Date
{{sentDate}}
§ 01Your Annual Statement

Dear {{participantName}},

Enclosed is your annual ESOP benefit statement for the {{planYear}} plan year. This statement summarizes the value of your ownership stake in {{companyName}} as of {{statementDate}}.

This is more than a piece of paper — it’s proof that your work at {{companyName}} is building real wealth for your future.

§ 02How to Read Your Statement

Your statement includes the following key items:

A.Total account balance. The fair market value of all shares allocated to your account as of the statement date.
B.Shares allocated. The number of shares of {{companyName}} stock held in your ESOP account.
C.Share value. The per-share fair market value, determined by an independent appraiser in accordance with ERISA requirements.
D.Vested balance. The portion of your account that you own outright. Unvested shares will be forfeited if you leave the company before your vesting date.
E.Year-over-year change. How your account value changed from the prior year, including new allocations and changes in share value.
§ 03Understanding Your Growth
Your Account at a Glance
{{priorYearBalance}}
{{newAllocations}}
{{valueChange}}
{{currentBalance}}

Your ESOP account grows in two ways: (1) new contributions allocated by the company, and (2) appreciation in the value of your existing shares. As {{companyName}} continues to grow and pay down debt, the per-share value is expected to increase over time.

§ 04Your Vesting Status
{{yearsOfService}}
{{vestingPercent}}
{{fullyVestedDate}}

You vest (earn full ownership of your shares) gradually over time. Once you are fully vested, the entire balance of your account is yours — even if you leave the company.

Important: Vesting is based on years of service, not years of participation. If you were employed before the ESOP was created, your prior service counts toward vesting.
§ 05Questions?

If you have questions about your statement, your vesting schedule, or the ESOP in general, please contact:

{{planAdminName}} — {{planAdminContact}}
{{hrContact}}

We encourage you to keep this statement with your other important financial documents.
